description_long: This is a vintage portable radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly rounded body made primarily of beige plastic. The front face features a large circular tuning dial with a metallic center and a clear frequency indicator, surrounded by a textured dark speaker grille. The top and bottom edges are ribbed for grip, and a maroon-brown handle arches over the top, attached at both sides for easy carrying. The back is smooth with a single screw and a dark horizontal accent, likely for battery access or ventilation. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century portable radios, with all controls and speaker output accessible from the front.
description: A compact, beige vintage portable radio with a large front tuning dial, dark speaker grille, and a maroon carrying handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large circular tuning dial, metallic center, and textured dark speaker grille.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the radio and the maroon handle attachment.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a smooth beige surface with a dark horizontal accent and a single screw.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle attachment and ribbed edge details.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
